_comment: Ground truth for the class-B (wrong-number) traps over seed.json. `grainSafeSql` is the correct query; `naiveSql` is the tempting fan-out/wrong-grain query that OVER-reports. class-b-ground-truth.test.ts executes both against a real SQL engine seeded from seed.json and asserts grainSafe === expectedRows and naive !== grainSafe. SQL is SQLite-dialect (booleans as 1) for the offline proof; the credentialed --execute eval uses LLM-generated SQL against the same data on any warehouse. expectedRows mirror the `expected.rows` in class-b-semantic.agent-evals.yml.

traps.name: revenue_per_customer_order_grain
traps.question: What is total order revenue for each customer?
traps.grainSafeSql: SELECT c.customer_name, SUM(o.order_total) AS revenue FROM fct_orders o JOIN dim_customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_name ORDER BY c.customer_name
traps.naiveSql: SELECT c.customer_name, SUM(o.order_total) AS revenue FROM fct_orders o JOIN dim_customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_name ORDER BY c.customer_name

traps.name: revenue_by_product_type
traps.question: Show revenue by product category
traps.grainSafeSql: SELECT product_type, SUM(product_price) AS revenue FROM order_items GROUP BY product_type ORDER BY product_type
traps.naiveSql: SELECT oi.product_type, SUM(oi.product_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN supplies s ON s.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY oi.product_type ORDER BY oi.product_type

traps.name: perishable_supply_revenue
traps.question: How much product revenue comes from perishable supplies?
traps.grainSafeSql: SELECT SUM(oi.product_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM supplies s WHERE s.product_id = oi.product_id AND s.is_perishable_supply = 1)
traps.naiveSql: SELECT SUM(oi.product_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN supplies s ON s.product_id = oi.product_id WHERE s.is_perishable_supply = 1
